execution_summary: The Docker environment was built (python:3.10-slim with pandas, numpy, scipy). Inside the container we executed replicate_analysis.py, which read the provided ‘ohtsubo data.csv’, created the reverse-coded intimacy scale, and performed a Welch two-sample t-test comparing the attention (n=21) and no-attention (n=20) groups. The analysis finished without errors, producing a t-statistic of 1.10 (p = 0.277) and Cohen’s d of 0.34. Thus, the observed effect is in the predicted positive direction but not statistically significant at p < .05, deviating from the preregistered success criterion. No deviations from the preregistered analytic plan were necessary.

code_executed.logs: Means Attention=22.86 (SD=6.04), Control=20.85 (SD=5.61); Welch t=1.102, p=0.277; Cohen’s d=0.34
code_executed.environment: Python 3.10 with pandas 1.5+, numpy 1.23+, scipy 1.9+ (Docker image python:3.10-slim)

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants who receive interpersonal attention will report higher summed intimacy scores than those who do not receive attention.
